title: podman top --latest generates error
body:
### Issue Description

podman top --help gives podman top --latest as a possible option. However, podman top --latest generates an error message:

```
C:\Users\Jeff>podman top --latest
Error: unknown flag: --latest
See 'podman top --help'
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman top --help
2. podman top --latest


### Describe the results you received

```
C:\Users\Jeff>podman top --latest
Error: unknown flag: --latest
See 'podman top --help'
```

### Describe the results you expected

Same as other options or help message updated

comments.body:
The `--latest` flag is not supported for remote clients (see https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-top.1.html#latest-l):
> [...]  (This option is not available with the remote Podman client, including Mac and Windows (excluding WSL2) machines)

But I understand the examples using -l/--latest are inviting to fall into this trap.
Please note that I am on WSL2 :)

> C:\Users\Jeff>podman top --latest

Are you really inside the Linux environment?
What does `podman info` spit out in the same terminal?

> > C:\Users\Jeff>podman top --latest
> 
> Are you really inside the Linux environment?

No that was from a Windows shell (thus remote)
I think at this point it would make the most sense to remove --latest from all our examples in the cli help and the docs.

> What does `podman info` spit out in the same terminal?

See above

> I think at this point it would make the most sense to remove --latest from all our examples in the cli help and the docs.

I agree.  I don't see another way to avoid this kind of confusion.
I agree remove it from examples in --help
can we close the issue since the PR was merged?
Yes, I think we can close.  `--latest` is still being used in examples in the man pages but there is a better explanation of `--latest` as well.
